nodes.title: Grad Seminar
nodes.description: Current problems and literature. Prerequisite: Chemistry Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Chemistry Graduate student. Graduate standing required

nodes.title: Gr Instrumental Analysis
nodes.description: Systematic survey of modern methods of chemical analysis. Fundamental principles and applications of spectroscopy, electrochemistry, and separation techniques. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 5320 and CHEM 3320. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 5320 and CHEM 3320.

nodes.title: Instrumental Analysis
nodes.description: Systematic survey of modern methods of chemical analysis. Fundamental principles and applications of spectroscopy, electrochemistry, and separation techniques. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 3320 and CHEM 5320. Prerequisite: CHEM 2310. Pre/Co-requisite: CHEM 2600 is strongly recommended.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Topics in Current Chemistry
nodes.description: Survey of current topics in the Chemistry literature. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Chemistry Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Chemistry Graduate student. Graduate standing required

nodes.title: Master's Lit Project Research
nodes.description: Reading and literature research culminating in the preparation of a comprehensive and critical review of a topic of current interest in Chemistry.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Physics II
nodes.description: Calculus-based introduction to electricity, magnetism and optics. For students in the natural sciences. Laboratory content is integrated with the lecture. Credit not awarded for both PHYS 1550 and PHYS 1650. Prerequisites: PHYS 1500 or PHYS 1600; credit or concurrent enrollment in MATH 1248.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both PHYS 1550 and PHYS 1650. Credit or concurrent enrollment in MATH 1248.

nodes.title: Exploring Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First-year laboratory addressing foundational chemical principles and experimental methods. For first-year Biochemistry majors also enrolled in CHEM 1500. Co-requisite: CHEM 1500.
nodes.other_requirements: For first-year Biochemistry majors also enrolled in CHEM 1500. Co-requisite: CHEM 1500. Coreq:CHEM 1500A; First-time, first-year BICM majors only; $85 fee for chemical lab supplies. PACE students by permission and override. The is the laboratory-component of General Chemistry for Biochemistry majors who have placed out of the lecture-component of General Chemistry based upon their High School preparation. The lab meets once per week. Students are expected to come prepared with proper PPE and write laboratory reports.

nodes.title: Discovering Chemistry
nodes.description: First-year Chemistry majors will gain an understanding of some of the core concepts and vocabulary in the discipline and be introduced to the scientific research going on in the department. Showcases academic and student services resources on campus. Provides an opportunity to meet and interact with other first-year students in the major.
nodes.other_requirements: FTFY CHEM majors only

nodes.title: Calculus II
nodes.description: Vectors and vector operations. Techniques and applications of integration. Polar coordinates, Taylor polynomials, sequences and series, power series. Credit will not be given for both MATH 1248 and MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 2248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1234. C- or better in MATH 021.

nodes.title: Outline: General Chem
nodes.description: One-semester survey of principles and concepts of general chemistry, topics covered include bonding, mole ratios, equilibrium, and nuclear chemistry. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1102 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1102 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405.

nodes.title: General Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Second semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include solutions, kinetics, equilibrium, acid-base chemistry, aqueous ionic equilibria, thermodynamics, electrochemistry, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1450 and CHEM 1455. Prerequisite: CHEM 1400 or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: $60 fee for lab supplies; Degree students only;

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry for Majors 1
nodes.description: An exploration of the basic principles of Organic Chemistry including structure, bonding, conformational analysis, stereochemistry and reactivity. Designed for Chemistry and Biochemistry majors who have a strong high school chemistry background.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override. CHEM and BICM majors only; $85 fee for lab supplies. Must register for CHEM 1500 lab L01-L05.

nodes.title: Gr Chemical Thermodynamics
nodes.description: Calculus-based exploration of the fundamental principles of thermodynamics (gases, equilibrium, free energy, laws of thermodynamics, statistical thermodynamics, phase transitions, mixtures, chemical reactions, solids), from an interdisciplinary perspective. This topic is a cornerstone of many scientific and engineering disciplines. Appropriate for students in Chemistry and other STEM fields. Prerequisites: Graduate student or Instructor permission; content knowledge of general chemistry, calculus, and introductory physics (mechanics) assumed. Cross-listed with: MATS 5610.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student or Instructor permission; content knowledge of general chemistry, calculus, and introductory physics (mechanics) assumed.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Physics I
nodes.description: Calculus-based introduction to kinematics, dynamics, oscillations, thermal physics. For students in the natural sciences. With lab. Credit not awarded for both PHYS 1600 and PHYS 1500. Pre/co-requisite: Credit or concurrent enrollment in MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit or concurrent enrollment in MATH 1234. Open to Degree and PACE students. Credit not awarded for both PHYS 1600 and PHYS 1500.

nodes.title: Professional Development
nodes.description: Skills necessary for senior Chemistry majors to transition to postgraduate careers, including resume and proposal writing, presentations, and other techniques. Prerequisite: CHEM 2012 or CHEM 2020.

nodes.title: Advanced Synthesis Techniques
nodes.description: Laboratory for Chemistry majors that covers advanced inorganic and organic techniques in synthesis, purification, and spectroscopic characterization. Prerequisite: CHEM 1550 or CHEM 2585.
nodes.other_requirements:
$230 fee for chemical lab supplies
PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Master's Lab Thesis Research
nodes.description: Research for the Master's Thesis.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing only

nodes.title: Doctoral Dissertation Research
nodes.description: Research for the Doctoral Dissertation.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing only

nodes.title: Quantum Mechanics and Kinetics
nodes.description: Exploration of chemical thermodynamics and basic statistical mechanics, with a significant level of mathematical rigor. Background in calculus and physics is required. Designed for Chemistry majors, but also suitable for students from other STEM disciplines. Prerequisites: CHEM 3610; PHYS 1550 or PHYS 1650. Pre/Co-requisites: MATH 2248.
nodes.other_requirements: Designed for Chemistry majors, but also suitable for students from other STEM disciplines.

nodes.title: Transitional Calculus
nodes.description: Intended to make the transition from MATH 1212 to MATH 2248. Topics are similar to MATH 1248 but recognizing different backgrounds of students in MATH 1212 versus MATH 1234. Credit will not be given for both MATH 1248 and MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 2248 or above. Prerequisite: B or better in MATH 1212, or B or better in MATH 1224.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Outline: Organic & BIOC w/lab
nodes.description: Broad overview of most important facts and principles of organic and biochemistry and interrelationships between these branches of chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1150 and CHEM 1152, CHEM 1580, or CHEM 1582. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a CHEM 1150 lab, L01 - L06; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies; Open to Degree and PACE students; Cross listed with CHEM 1152A; total enrollment cap = 142

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 1
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of organic compounds with consideration of bonding, stereochemistry, and reaction mechanisms. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2580 and CHEM 1580, CHEM 1582, or CHEM 1500. Prerequisite: CHEM 1450 or CHEM 1455.
nodes.other_requirements: $85 fee for lab supplies; Must register for a CHEM 2580 lab L01-L23; Degree students only; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2580 ZRA-ZRC

nodes.title: Gr Advanced Organic Chem 1
nodes.description: Stereochemistry, conformational analysis, stereoelectronic effects, transition state theory, molecular orbital theory, and reactivity criteria are discussed in regards to reaction mechanisms and functional group manipulations. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 5580 and CHEM 4580. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student

nodes.title: Physics for Engineers I
nodes.description: Mechanics including oscillations and waves. With lab. Accompanying optional problem-solving session: PHYS 1510. Prerequisite: MATH 1234 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: School of Engineering students only: CEMS-undeclared, BIOM, CE, EE, EENV, EMGT, ME, CS, DS, MATH; Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Chemical Thermodynamics
nodes.description: Calculus-based exploration of the fundamental principles of thermodynamics (gases, equilibrium, free energy, laws of thermodynamics, statistical thermodynamics, phase transitions, mixtures, chemical reactions), from both a chemistry and physics perspective. This topic is a cornerstone of many scientific and engineering disciplines. Appropriate for students in Chemistry and other STEM fields. Prerequisites: CHEM 1070, CHEM 1450, or CHEM 1455; MATH 1224, MATH 1248, or MATH 1242; PHYS 1450, PHYS 1550, or PHYS 1650. Co-requisite: CHEM 3602.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override. Co-requisite: CHEM 3602.

nodes.title: Physical Chem for Life Science
nodes.description: Introduction to physical chemistry concepts spanning thermodynamics, solution equilibrium, enzyme kinetics, and other topics. Appropriate for students from Biochemistry and other life science disciplines. Background in calculus and physics is required. Prerequisites: CHEM 1450, CHEM 1455, or CHEM 1460; MATH 1224, MATH 1248, or MATH 1242; PHYS 1400, PHYS 1500, or PHYS 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: Background in calculus and physics is required. PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate students work on research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisite: Departmental permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Departmental permission.

nodes.title: Physical Chemistry Preparation
nodes.description: Review of relevant mathematical and physical concepts as applied to physical chemistry. Prerequisites: CHEM 1450 or CHEM 1455 or CHEM 1460; MATH 1248.

nodes.title: Intro Organic Chemistry w/lab
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of basic organic compounds of technological and biological significance. Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1152, CHEM 1582, CHEM 1500, or CHEM 2580. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1580 and  CHEM 1150 ,  CHEM 1152 ,  CHEM 1582 ,  CHEM 1500 , or  CHEM 2580 . Must register for a CHEM 1580 lab, L01-L15; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies; Cross listed withCHEM 1582A. PACE students with permission and override. All labs must be completed in order to pass the course. Attendance for all lectures and exams is expected. All work must be completed by the due date. The final exam must be taken to pass the course.

nodes.title: Intro Organic Chemistry
nodes.description: Properties and reactivity of organic molecules of technological and biological significance. No laboratory. Not recommended for pre-medical students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1582 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1152, CHEM 1580, CHEM 1500, or CHEM 2580. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100 or CHEM 1102 or CHEM 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Organometallic Chemistry
nodes.description: Synthesis, structure, bonding, properties, reactions, and applications of organometallic systems; mechanisms of organometallic reactions including oxidative addition and insertion reactions with applications in catalysis. Prerequisite: Chemistry Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Chemistry Graduate student.

nodes.title: Independent Study
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Elementary Physics II
nodes.description: Algebra-based survey of electricity, magnetism, optics and modern physics. Appropriate for students in health and life sciences. Counts as a lecture and lab. Optional accompanying problem solving recitation: PHYS 1460. Prerequisites: PHYS 1400, PHYS 1500, or PHYS 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for labs L01 - L03; Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus II
nodes.description: Techniques and applications of integration. An introduction to multi-variable calculus: partial derivatives and double integrals. Prerequisite: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Exploring Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Second semester of a first-year laboratory addressing foundational chemical principles and experimental methods. For first-year Biochemistry majors also enrolled in CHEM 1550. Prerequisites: CHEM 1500 and CHEM 1410. Co-requisite: CHEM 1550.
nodes.other_requirements: CHEM 1550; BICM majors only; $85 Fee: Chem Lab Supplies PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Outline: Organic & BIOC
nodes.description: Broad overview of most important facts and principles of organic and biochemistry and of interrelationships between these branches of chemistry. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1152 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1580, or CHEM 1582. Prerequisite: CHEM 1100, CHEM 1102, or CHEM 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1152 and CHEM 1150, CHEM 1580, or CHEM 1582.

nodes.title: Analytical Spectroscopy
nodes.description: Selected topics of current interest in analytical chemistry. New techniques and methodologies, especially in chemical instrumentation. See Schedule of Courses for specific titles. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate standing required

nodes.title: Research Problem Conception
nodes.description: Identification of a current research problem to be addressed by original, independent research. Prerequisite: Chemistry Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Chemistry Graduate student. Graduate standing only

nodes.title: Gr Advanced Inorganic Chem
nodes.description: Molecular symmetry and group theory with an emphasis on applications (vibrational and electronic spectra, bonding and reactivity); introduction to transition metal processes; bioinorganic chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 5400 and CHEM 3400. Prerequisite: Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Graduate student. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 5400 and CHEM 3400.

nodes.title: Communication Skills Seminar
nodes.description: Seminar focusing on information literacy, writing, and presentation skills relevant to the chemical sciences. Students will use discipline-specific search tools and evaluate primary literature to complete written and oral assignments. Prerequisite: CHEM 1550 or CHEM 2585.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Instrumental Analysis Lab
nodes.description: Laboratory for undergraduates following CHEM 3320. Application of chemical and physical principles to qualitative and quantitative chemical problems. Study of the interplay of data, hypotheses, and hypothesis-driven experimentation through application of the scientific method. Prerequisites: CHEM 3320.
nodes.other_requirements:
Instructor permission required
PACE students by permission and override
Fee: $255

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry for Majors 2
nodes.description: A survey of the reactivity of organic functional groups from a mechanistic standpoint. Organic synthesis will be emphasized. Prerequisite: CHEM 1500 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a CHEM 1550 lab, L01-L04; CHEM or BICM majors only; $85 fee: Chem Lab Supplies. PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Computational Chemistry
nodes.description: Explores the techniques and applications of computational chemistry to model organic, inorganic, and biological molecules. Introduces basic level of classical and quantum modeling, cheminformatics and big chemical data, as well as computer-aided design of new materials and medicines. Prerequisite: Chemistry Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Chemistry Graduate student. Graduate standing required

nodes.title: Physics for Engineers II
nodes.description: Electricity, magnetism, electromagnetic waves, optics. Without lab. Accompanying optional problem-solving session: PHYS 1560. Prerequisites: PHYS 1500; MATH 1248 or MATH 1242. Co-requisite: MATH 2248.
nodes.other_requirements: CEMS students only

nodes.title: General Chemistry 1
nodes.description: First semester of a two-semester sequence. Topics include matter, stoichiometry, gas laws, thermochemistry, quantum theory, atomic structure, electronic configurations, bonding, and intermolecular forces. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1400 and CHEM 1100, CHEM 1102, or CHEM 1405.

nodes.title: Physical Chemistry Lab
nodes.description: Laboratory course following CHEM 2600. Topics include quantum chemistry and thermodynamics. Prerequisites: CHEM 1550 or CHEM 2585; CHEM 2600.

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Calculus III
nodes.description: Vector-valued functions. Calculus of functions of several variables: partial derivatives, gradient, divergence, curl, multiple integrals, line integrals, Stokes' and Green's theorems. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1248 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1248 or MATH 1242

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry 2
nodes.description: Reactivity of organic compounds and applications to synthesis. Spectroscopy is discussed in relation to compound characterization. For premedical and biological sciences students. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 2585 and CHEM 1550. Prerequisite: CHEM 1500 or CHEM 2580.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for a CHEM 2585 lab, L01-L20; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; Post Bac Pre Med students register for CHEM 2585 ZRA; Fee: $85

nodes.title: Research Problem Solution
nodes.description: Solution to a current research problem to be addressed by original, independent research. Prerequisite: CHEM 7010.
nodes.other_requirements: Meeting time/day set in collaboration with the students and instructor at the beginning of the term.

nodes.title: Outline: General Chem w/lab
nodes.description: One-semester survey of principles and concepts of general chemistry, topics covered include bonding, mole ratios, equilibrium, and nuclear chemistry. Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1102, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit not awarded for both CHEM 1100 and CHEM 1102, CHEM 1400, or CHEM 1405. $60 fee for lab supplies; Colocated with CHEM 1102A; Must register for CHEM 1100 lab L01-L07; Non-CAS, non - BSAD students only; Open to degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Quantitative Analysis
nodes.description: Theory and practice of volumetric and gravimetric analysis. Theoretical discussion of indicators, buffers, pH, etc. Introduction to data analysis, spectrophotometry, and chromatography. Prerequisite: CHEM 1450 or CHEM 1455 or CHEM 1460.
nodes.other_requirements: must register for CHEM 2310 lab L01-L03; $65 fee for lab supplies; PACE students by permission and override

nodes.title: Intro to Graduate Research
nodes.description: Introduction to graduate research in Chemistry. Overview of faculty research areas and department/university research resources. Prerequisite: Chemistry Graduate student.
nodes.other_requirements: Chemistry Graduate student. Graduate standing required

nodes.title: Gr Special Topics
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Elementary Physics I
nodes.description: Algebra-based survey of mechanics, oscillations, waves and thermal physics. Appropriate for students in health and life sciences. Counts as lecture and lab. Optional accompanying problem solving recitation section: PHYS 1410. Prerequisites: High school algebra and trigonometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for PHYS 1400 L01-L04; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Inorganic Chemistry
nodes.description: Symmetry, group theory, molecular structure; electronic structure of atoms; bonding models including MO, crystal field, and ligand field; solid state, acid-base, and simple organometallic systems. Prerequisite: CHEM 1500 or CHEM 2580.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override
